title: Golden Glazed Argentinian Kabocha Gratin
description: A nutty and creamy Argentinian dish featuring kabocha, lacquer-glaze, and a unique agave-leaf vessel, this recipe is egg-free-friendly and perfect for a special occasion. The combination of flavors and textures creates a nostalgic and savory experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Prepare the agave leaves by cleaning and drying them thoroughly.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the cubed kabocha squash to the skillet and cook until tender, about 10 minutes.
In a separate saucepan, combine the heavy cream and lacquer glaze, and stir over low heat until the glaze is fully incorporated.
Remove the squash mixture from the skillet and transfer it to the agave leaves, arranging it in a single layer.
Pour the cream and glaze mixture over the squash, making sure it is fully coated.
Sprinkle the grated parmesan cheese over the top of the squash.
Place the agave leaves in a baking dish and bake for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
